seoTitle: Mount Cooper vs Mount David — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Cooper and Mount David. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Cooper vs Mount David: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Cooper and Mount David.
heading: Mount Cooper vs Mount David — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Cooper, New South Wales and Mount David, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount David has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Mount Cooper (47/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Cooper or Mount David safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Cooper has a flood risk score of 48/100, while Mount David scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Cooper scores 56/100 for bushfire risk. Mount David scores 47/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Cooper or Mount David?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount David has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.55% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
